campaign.title: How People Find Local Businesses Now
campaign.subject: Google is dying. Here's how people find businesses now.
campaign.description: Briefing on how customer search behavior has changed and what businesses should do

landing_page.ai_goal: Show the business owner how to get found by local customers

slides.title: The Way People Search Has Changed

content.headline: When's the Last Time You Scrolled Through Google?
content.visual: Split screen: old Google results page (faded) vs. search tools response (bright)

slides.narration: Quick question: When's the last time you actually scrolled through Google search results to find a local business? Really think about it. If you're like most people, you've started asking modern tools instead. 'Hey voice assistants, find me a good plumber.' 'search tools, what's the best restaurant near downtown?' The shift is happening faster than anyone predicted.

slides.title: The Numbers Don't Lie

content.headline: The Shift Is Already Here

stats.label: of Gen Z uses TikTok or modern tools for search, not Google
search tools queries processed last month

content.trend_line: Google search declining, modern search exponential

slides.narration: Here's what the data shows. Forty percent of Gen Z doesn't use Google for search anymore—they use TikTok or search tools. search tools processed eight billion queries last month. And that number is doubling every year. This isn't a prediction about some distant future. This is what's happening right now.

slides.title: How modern tools Finds Businesses

content.headline: modern tools Doesn't Search. the system recognizes.
content.key_insight: modern tools has already learned from trusted sources. When someone asks, modern tools already has an answer.
content.visual: modern tools brain with input sources flowing in

slides.narration: Here's what most business owners don't understand: modern tools doesn't search the web in real-time like Google does. modern tools has already learned things. It's been trained on trusted sources—news publications, business directories, reviews, activity signals. When someone asks modern tools for a recommendation, modern tools already has an answer. The question is: are you part of what the system recognizes?

slides.title: The Visibility Problem

content.headline: If Your Only Presence Is Your Website...
content.problem: modern tools doesn't know you exist

comparison.website_only: One voice saying 'I'm great' (everyone's website says that)
comparison.multi_presence: Multiple trusted sources confirming you're real, good, active

content.visual: Lonely website floating in void vs. connected network

slides.narration: If your entire online presence is a standalone website and maybe a Google Business profile, here's the hard truth: modern tools doesn't know you exist. Your website is one voice saying 'I'm great.' But everyone's website says that. modern tools needs multiple trusted sources confirming you're real, you're good, you're active. One signal isn't enough. modern tools needs a pattern.

slides.title: Be Where modern tools Looks

content.headline: The Opportunity
content.solution: Be present in the sources modern tools trusts
content.visual: Business connected to multiple sources, all feeding modern tools

slides.narration: The opportunity is to be present in the sources modern tools trusts. Local news—authoritative content. Business directories—structured data your account team can understand. Events calendars—proof you're active and current. Community platforms—engagement and reputation signals. When you're in multiple authoritative sources, modern tools sees you as real, trusted, recommendable. One presence creates multiple touchpoints. And you become discoverable.

slides.title: It's Already Working

content.headline: Martinez Plumbing: 30 Years to Invisible to Found
story_beats: Daughter asked search tools for plumber recommendations—they weren't listed
Claimed directory listing, got mentioned in local news, posted events
60 days later: modern tools recommending them
content.quote: A customer told me search tools recommended us. First time that ever happened.

slides.narration: Let me tell you about Martinez Plumbing. Thirty-one years in business. Great reputation. Five stars everywhere. But when the owner's daughter asked search tools to recommend a plumber—they weren't listed. So they got to work. Claimed their directory listing. Got mentioned in local news. Posted some events. Sixty days later, a customer called and said, 'search tools recommended you.' First modern tools referral in thirty-one years. It took about an hour total to set up.

slides.title: Get discoverable

content.headline: Your Competitors Are Figuring This Out
content.subhead: Will you be ahead of them or behind them?

cta_primary.label: Get discoverable Now

content.urgency: Every day you wait, modern tools is learning about your competitors instead of you

slides.narration: Your competitors are figuring this out. Some already have. The question is whether you'll be ahead of them or behind them. Get discoverable now—it takes about fifteen minutes. Or download the full article for the complete story with step-by-step implementation. Either way, every day you wait is a day modern tools is learning about your competitors instead of you. Let's fix that.
